mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers corporate margin compression and negative equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.8 percent in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicates a supply-demand mismatch that drains consumer spending power, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 0.5 percent month-over-month while RSAFS declines more than 1.5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline simultaneously, or the joint signal predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained drop in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader credit contraction and consumer confidence collapse, depressing equity valuations over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower following housing start declines when the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, or the conditional alpha drops below zero in a 12-month rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 205.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while real personal savings rates fall indicates wage-driven consumption fueled by debt, which historically precedes a credit crunch and negative equity returns over a five-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises more than 150 thousand month-over-month while RSAFS falls more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both proxies diverge as specified, or the joint signal predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated policy rates, a sharp rebound in industrial production signals successful supply-side normalization that eases input cost pressures, boosting corporate margins and driving positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher following industrial production rebounds when the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, or the conditional alpha drops below zero in a 12-month rolling test.
